title: Modi and Merz Seal Defence Roadmap at G7, Mark 75 Years of India-Germany Ties
lede: Prime Minister Modi and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz signed a Defence Industrial Cooperation Roadmap during their second bilateral meeting of 2026 at the G7 Summit in Evian, cementing momentum in the India-Germany Strategic Partnership as both nations mark 75 years of diplomatic relations.

sections.context: The Modi-Merz meeting on 17 June 2026 represents the second high-level engagement between the two leaders within a single calendar year, signalling accelerated diplomatic cadence between New Delhi and Berlin. This acceleration follows Chancellor Merz's successful visit to India earlier in 2026, during which both sides concluded negotiations on the India-EU Free Trade Agreement—a landmark commercial framework. The timing at the G7 Summit in Evian places the bilateral partnership within a multilateral context, suggesting India-Germany cooperation now intersects with broader Western security and economic coordination. The 75th anniversary of India-Germany diplomatic relations in 2026 provides the symbolic backdrop for renewed institutional commitments across defence, trade, technology, and sustainable development.
sections.threads: The Defence Industrial Cooperation Roadmap operationalises a strategic pivot first articulated in [[india-de-modis-berlin-blueprint-how-india-germany-locked-in-a-whole-o-2015|India and Germany's earlier strategic frameworks]]. It extends Germany's manufacturing and engineering expertise—evident in [[india-de-german-manufacturing-muscle-enters-indias-electrification-ra-2023|previous cooperation on electrification projects]]—into the defence-industrial sector. The operationalisation of a transit visa waiver for Indian nationals through Germany reflects broader labour mobility initiatives that [[india-de-germany-unlocks-skilled-labour-corridors-as-berlin-recalibra-2024|Berlin has recalibrated in recent years]]. The FTA conclusion signals economic deepening alongside security cooperation, positioning the bilateral relationship across both hard and soft power domains within the G7 framework.
sections.signal: Analysts should monitor the operationalisation timeline and scope of the Defence Industrial Cooperation Roadmap over the next 12–18 months. Key decision points include: sectoral prioritisation (aerospace, naval, land systems), technology transfer frameworks, and joint production arrangements. The successful Merz visit and G7 meeting establish conditions for a potential reciprocal Modi visit to Germany or further Merz travel to India, which would signal sustained momentum. Watch for India-EU FTA ratification processes and how defence cooperation integrates with broader EU strategic autonomy discussions. The visa waiver's practical implementation will indicate whether mobility frameworks can scale beyond transit arrangements into broader people-to-people engagement.
